title: eriogonum kelloggii
intro: Eriogonum kelloggii, also known as Kellogg's buckwheat, is a perennial flowering plant native to the Pacific coast of North America, characterized by its white or pink flowers and small, oval-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Eriogonum kelloggii include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support the immune system, making it a popular herb for natural remedies. Eriogonum kelloggii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or topical cream, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote skin health and reduce inflammation.
